# Eclipse Ankaios

Eclipse Ankaios is a lightweight workload orchestrator for embedded and automotive edge devices.
It manages containerized workloads across one server and multiple agents running on resource-constrained hardware.
Use this documentation to understand the architecture, configure manifests, interact with the Control Interface, and contribute to the project.


## Overview

- [Welcome](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/index.md)
- [Architecture](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/architecture/index.md)

## Usage

- [Installation](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/installation/index.md)
- [Quickstart](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/quickstart/index.md)
- [Tutorial: Registering for events](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/tutorial-events/index.md)
- [Tutorial: Manage a fleet of vehicles from the cloud](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/tutorial-fleet-management/index.md)
- [Tutorial: Sending and receiving vehicle signals](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/tutorial-vehicle-signals/index.md)
- [Awesome Ankaios](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/awesome-ankaios/index.md)
- [Setting up Ankaios with mTLS](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/mtls-setup/index.md)
- [Shell completion](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/shell-completion/index.md)
- [Config objects](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/manifest/config-objects/index.md)
- [Workload files](https://eclipse-ankaios.github.io/ankaios/main/usage/manifest/workload-files/index.md)
